title: Medical Office Replacement Sourcing
name: Medical Office Replacement Sourcing
metaTitle: Medical Office Replacement Sourcing in Seattle, WA
metaDescription: Medical office replacement sourcing for Seattle 1031 exchanges, scoped like a bid package and tracked against your 45-day identification window.

sections.html: Investors exchanging into medical office space need a sourcing process that behaves like a bid package, not a broker flyer. In the Seattle market, this office assembles a scoped submittal of outpatient and clinical assets, line-itemed and reviewed before a single property reaches your identification file.

sections.heading: Scope of the Sourcing Engagement
sections.html:
The engagement starts with a written scope: asset type, price band, submarket radius, and the tenant profile you will accept. That scope becomes the filter for every candidate we bring forward, so a Seattle search stays disciplined instead of opportunistic, and you are not reviewing properties that never had a chance of clearing your criteria.

Each candidate that clears the filter is packaged the way a general contractor packages a bid: a cover sheet describing the asset, a set of exhibits pulled from the seller's file, and a sign-off line before it moves to your review. Nothing reaches your desk as a raw listing.

Investors receive the identical package format whether the candidate is a single suite conversion or a full medical building, so a Seattle buyer comparing several candidates side by side is comparing apples to apples rather than reconciling different broker formats, spreadsheet layouts, and inconsistent labeling from one offering memorandum to the next.

sections.heading: Where Outpatient Product Sits in the Seattle Market
sections.html:
First Hill still holds the deepest concentration of hospital-adjacent medical office in Seattle, with older buildings that carry seismic retrofit exposure investors need flagged early rather than discovered at inspection. The Eastside, closer to Bellevue and Redmond employment, has produced newer outpatient buildings built alongside the tech-campus growth tied to Amazon's broader footprint in the region.

Further south, the Kent Valley bench offers lower basis per square foot for investors trading down in size but up in yield, though tenant density there skews toward urgent care and imaging suites rather than full health-system anchor leases.

A smaller but steady pocket of wellness and specialty-clinic space has followed the South Lake Union tech workforce, since a dense daytime population supports urgent, drop-in style clinical use even where full hospital-system anchors are absent.

sections.heading: Package Line Items Verified Before Submittal
sections.html:
Before a candidate is added to your file, our team checks the following against the offering package:

  • Remaining lease term and renewal options measured against your closing timeline
  • Tenant credit and health-system affiliation, where one exists
  • Parking ratio measured against exam-room count, not gross square footage
  • Seismic retrofit status for any structure built before current code
  • ADA and life-safety compliance history pulled from the seller's file
  • CAM reconciliation records for the trailing two years

sections.heading: Coordinating the Submittal Against Your Exchange Clock
sections.html:
Every candidate package is timestamped against your 45-day identification window, not delivered on a general timeline. If a property needs a site visit or a specialist inspection before it can be added to your notice, that step is scheduled early enough to leave room for a substitute candidate if the visit turns up a problem.

We route the finished package to your qualified intermediary once you have signed off, and we do not advise on the tax treatment of the exchange itself. Investors confirm basis and gain calculations with their own tax advisor before funds move.

Where financing is involved, we also confirm the lender's own timeline for appraisal and underwriting against the same clock, so a slow appraisal on one candidate does not quietly consume days that should be spent on the identification notice itself.

sections.heading: Handoff to Closing
sections.html:
Once a candidate is selected, the package transfers intact to your closing team: lease abstract, tenant estoppel status, and the diligence log built during sourcing. Nothing is re-created at the closing table because the exhibits already exist in the file, which keeps the last mile of the exchange short and predictable.

A short transition memo covering tenant contacts, key holders, and any open maintenance items also accompanies the file, so day-one ownership of a Seattle medical building does not start with a search for basic building information.

faqs.q: What does a medical office sourcing package include for a Seattle exchange?
faqs.a: It includes a cover sheet describing the asset, a lease abstract, a tenant credit summary, and a diligence log covering seismic, ADA, and CAM history. The package is built to be handed directly to your closing team without rework.

faqs.q: How do you handle older Seattle buildings that may need seismic retrofit work?
faqs.a: We flag retrofit exposure during initial screening and price it into the review before the property is added to your file. Investors decide whether to accept that exposure or move to the next candidate.

faqs.q: Can the search include Eastside or Kent Valley submarkets beyond Seattle proper?
faqs.a: Yes. The sourcing scope is set by asset type and price band first, so Eastside and Kent Valley candidates are included whenever they fit the criteria set at the start of the engagement.

faqs.q: Who reviews the lease abstract before it becomes part of my exchange file?
faqs.a: Our team reviews it against the original lease and any amendments before it is added to the package. Investors and their advisors receive the finished abstract, not a raw copy of the seller's paperwork.

faqs.q: Does this service replace advice from my CPA or qualified intermediary?
faqs.a: No. We coordinate the sourcing and diligence package only. Tax treatment, basis calculations, and the exchange agreement itself stay with your qualified intermediary and tax advisor.
